item.title: Vitamin A crisis yet to be resolved
item.description: The government has yet to resolve shortages of vitamin A capsules and contraceptives because of complications in the procurement system, officials said on Wednesday, as eight more children died and 1,615 others were infected in the ongoing measles outbreak in the 24 hours until 8:00am......

item.title: 12 burnt in Sonargaon power plant canteen gas explosion
item.description: Twelve people suffered burn injuries in an explosion reportedly triggered by accumulated gas at the canteen of the JERA Meghnaghat Power Limited in Narayanganj’s Sonargaon upazila on Wednesday afternoon......

item.title: Minister seeks Jamaat’s cooperation to resist comeback of fascist rule
item.description: Bangladesh Nationalist Party leader and water resources minister Shahid Uddin Chowdhury Anee on Wednesday sought the cooperation of the Bangladesh Jamaat-e-Islami to resist the comeback of fascist rule in the country......Bangladesh Travel Guide

item.title: 2 businesses fined for selling unauthorised cosmetics
item.description: Two establishments in Rajshahi city were fined on Wednesday for selling cosmetics without BSTI approval and past their expiry dates......

item.title: Govt appoints 96 more from 27th BCS after 19 years
item.description: The government on Wednesday appointed 96 more candidates to different cadres under the 27th Bangladesh Civil Service examination, nearly 19 years after they had been deprived from joining despite passing the examination......

item.title: 4 held with fake notes worth Tk 5 lakh in Sylhet
item.description: Four alleged members of a counterfeit currency note racket were arrested in Sylhet early Wednesday, with counterfeit notes worth Tk 5,02,700 recovered from their possession......

item.title: Hypertension taking heavy toll amid lack of sustainable financing
item.description: Health rights activists and public health experts on Wednesday said that hypertension was taking a heavy toll in Bangladesh due to inadequate and unsustainable financing for prevention and treatment services......

item.title: Court orders PBI to investigate case over attack on lawyers
item.description: A metropolitan magistrate court in Chattogram on Wednesday ordered the Police Bureau of Investigation to investigate a case filed over attacks on lawyers during the violence in the court area on the day advocate Saiful Islam Alif was killed in November 2024......

item.title: Sheba Prokashoni suspends operations
item.description: Popular publishing house Sheba Prokashoni on Wednesday issued a notice suspending all activities over allegations of internal corruption and irregularities......

item.title: Collagraph prints exhibition begins at Kaalnee Art Studio
item.description: A ten-day print exhibition titled ‘Collagraph Printmaking Exhibition’ opened on Wednesday at the Kaalnee Art Studio at Rayerbazar in Dhaka......Geographic Reference

item.title: CMU celebrates University Day
item.description: The Chittagong Medical University has organised a five-day programme, celebrating University Day, inaugurated at the CMU on Tuesday......

item.title: Coast Guard arrests ‘drug dealer’ at Teknaf
item.description: The Bangladesh Coast Guard arrested a drug dealer with 7,000 Yaba pills worth Tk 35 lakh at Teknaf in Cox’s Bazar on Tuesday......

item.title: Khulna JCD convener removed hours after announcement
item.description: Bangladesh Jatiyatabadi Chhatra Dal on Wednesday removed newly appointed Khulna district unit convener Firoz Ahmed from his organisational post within 15 hours of announcing the committee and appointed senior joint convener Abu Jafar as acting convener......

item.title: Woman gets life term for killing twin daughters
item.description: A court in Khulna on Wednesday sentenced a woman to life imprisonment for killing her twin daughters in a widely discussed murder case in Terokhada upazila......

item.title: Govt, private initiatives vital to boost livestock sector: minister
item.description: State minister for fisheries and livestock Sultan Salauddin Tuku on Wednesday emphasised that the livestock sector could be further enriched through the combined efforts of the government and private entrepreneurs......

item.title: Untimely rainfall damages boro harvest in Jhenaidah
item.description: CONTINUOUS rainfall over the past several days has severely disrupted Boro paddy harvesting across Jhenaidah, leaving vast areas of ripe crops submerged and creating growing concern among farmers over financial losses and a possible cattle fodder crisis......

item.title: Three pastors gunned down in Manipur
item.description: Three pastors were gunned down Wednesday in India’s restive northeastern Manipur state, police and officials said, the latest flare-up in a region gripped by ethnic conflict......

item.title: Vijay wins floor test with 144 votes
item.description: ‘Thalapathy’ Vijay is safe, for now. The superstar actor — who upended Tamil Nadu politics by leading his Tamilaga Vettri Kazhagam to victory in last month’s election, breaking the DMK and AIADMK’s 62-year stranglehold on the state’spoliticallandscape — was confirmed Wednesday as the new chief minister after winning a dramatic trust vote, reports NDTV......Politics

item.title: Jamaat spent Tk 4.49cr, Jatiya Party Tk 5 lakh in polls
item.description: Bangladesh Jamaat-e-Islami spent over Tk 4.49 crore in the 13th parliamentary election, while Jatiya Party reported spending only Tk 5 lakh, according to official election expenditure statements submitted to the election commission......

item.title: UGC nat’l workshop emphasises on research, innovation, skill development
item.description: Educationists, researchers, industry representatives, policymakers and representatives of civil society at a national workshop in Dhaka on Tuesday put forward a set of recommendations with a view to sustainable transformation of the country’s higher education system......

item.title: Nawapara traders observe strike over fellow’s killing
item.description: Loading and unloading of imported goods, including rice, coal, fertiliser, cement and stone, were suspended on Wednesday as traders enforced a dawn-to-dusk general strike, protesting at the killing of a fellow businessman......

item.title: BNP leader, three others sued over looting of Tk 50 lakh cumin in Rajshahi
item.description: A case was filed on Tuesday night against a local Bangladesh Nationalist Party leader and three others over the alleged looting of a truck carrying 300 sacks of cumin worth nearly Tk 50 lakh at Bagha upazila in Rajshahi......Bangladesh Travel Guide

item.title: Nationally productive and globally competitive
item.description: HIGHER education reform requires genuine, unwavering and strongpoliticalwill, the highest level of commitment from the government and the national leadership. Reform also demands capable and visionary academic leadership of international standing, supported by sufficient investment in quality education and innovative research......Politics

item.title: Gas shortages and policy failures
item.description: ONCE, gas was considered an almost inexhaustible resource in Bangladesh. People used to dry clothes over gas stoves, keep burners running all day and pay only a nominal monthly bill. Illegal connections were, in many cases, not even considered a serious social offence, because there was a firm belief that this gas would never run out. But that belief has now been shattered......

item.title: Forgotten lessons in our schools
item.description: IN Bangladesh, education is often treated as the primary route to stability, dignity and social mobility. Parents invest enormous financial and emotional energy into their children’s schooling, students spend years preparing for examinations and academic achievement continues to shape how success is measured in society. From early childhood, students are trained......

item.title: Economics of carbon sequestration
item.description: CARBON sequestration was once treated largely as a technical scientific issue discussed in climate summits, research institutions and environmental negotiations. Today, it is becoming something far more immediate: an economic question with direct implications for development, public finance and national survival. As climate change intensifies and global carbon......

item.title: Govt should practise rather than preach on univ issues
item.description: THE remark of the prime minister, Tarique Rahman, of May 12 that the University of Dhaka fails to appear in international rankings as teacher appointment has for long been influenced by political considerations rather than merit is a welcome observation. The prime minister made the remark in an interactive session at the social science faculty of the university after he had......Maps

item.title: Increase in sexual violence must be arrested
item.description: A SHARP increase in sexual violence against children has been reported in early 2026, highlighting a deepening crisis. Rights group Ain O Salish Kendra’s data show that at least 180 rape cases were recorded in the first four months of the year and 81 of victims were under 18. Fifty-six of them were, alarmingly, girls aged below 12, including children under six. Eighteen rape......

item.title: Scientists decipher rules of mosquito attraction
item.description: A range of sensory cues can cause mosquitoes to pick one human over another — mainly the smell and heat our bodies give off, and the carbon dioxide we exhale......
